---
title: "Differentiation Progression Analysis"
subtitle: >
  Quantifying differentiation timing differences
  from bulk RNA-seq time course data
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    fig-width: 8
    fig-height: 6
    fig-dpi: 150
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Overview

This workflow quantifies how differentiation timing
differs between experimental conditions using bulk
RNA-seq data. It identifies temporally variable genes
via the likelihood ratio test, constructs a reference
trajectory through PCA space using control samples, and
projects all samples onto that trajectory to compute a
scalar maturation score.

![](assets/overview.svg){width="100%"}

**Dataset.** Publicly available data from
[Martinez et al. (2024)](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.stemcr.2024.04.010):
D21 (euploid control) and T21 (trisomy 21) iPSC-derived
neural progenitors sampled at Days 6, 10, and 17 of
differentiation. 18 samples total (3 replicates per
genotype per timepoint).

## Workflow

![](assets/workflow-flowchart.svg){width="50%"
fig-align="center"}

## Mathematical Framework

### Negative Binomial GLM

DESeq2 models read counts with a negative binomial
distribution, where each gene $i$ in sample $j$ follows:

$$
K_{ij} \sim \text{NB}\!\left(\mu_{ij},\; \alpha_i\right)
$$

$$
\log_2(\mu_{ij}) = \sum_r \beta_r^{(i)}\, x_{jr}
$$

where $\alpha_i$ is the gene-specific dispersion and
$x_{jr}$ are design matrix entries.

### Likelihood Ratio Test

The LRT compares a **full** model (including timepoint)
against a **reduced** model (intercept only):

$$
D = -2\left[
  \ell\!\left(\hat{\boldsymbol{\theta}}_{\text{reduced}}\right)
  - \ell\!\left(\hat{\boldsymbol{\theta}}_{\text{full}}\right)
\right]
\;\sim\; \chi^2_{df_{\text{full}} - df_{\text{reduced}}}
$$

Genes with significant $D$ statistics (after BH
correction) change expression across timepoints.

### PCA

Given a centered expression matrix $\mathbf{X}_c$
(genes $\times$ samples), PCA computes the singular
value decomposition:

$$
\mathbf{X}_c = \mathbf{U}\boldsymbol{\Sigma}\mathbf{V}^\top
$$

Each sample's coordinates in PC space:

$$
\mathbf{z}_i
= \mathbf{V}^\top
  \!\left(\mathbf{x}_i - \bar{\mathbf{x}}\right)
$$

### Centroid Vector Projection

The reference vector connects control centroids at the
earliest and latest timepoints:

$$
\vec{v}
= \bar{\mathbf{z}}_{t_{\text{late}}}
  - \bar{\mathbf{z}}_{t_{\text{early}}}
\qquad\text{where}\quad
\bar{\mathbf{z}}_t
= \frac{1}{n_t}\sum_{i \in t}\mathbf{z}_i
$$

### Maturation Score

Each sample is projected onto the reference vector and
normalized so the early centroid maps to 0 and the late
centroid maps to 1:

$$
s_i
= \frac{
    \left(\mathbf{z}_i
      - \bar{\mathbf{z}}_{t_{\text{early}}}\right)
    \cdot \vec{v}
  }{
    \lVert\vec{v}\rVert^2
  }
$$

Scores below 0 indicate less-differentiated-than-early
control; scores above 1 indicate
more-differentiated-than-late control.

---

## Analysis

### Setup

```{r}
#| label: setup
library(DESeq2)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
library(plotly)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)

meta <- readRDS("dat/metadata/WC24_metadata_clean.rds")
raw_counts <- readRDS(
  "dat/counts/raw/WC24_filt_raw_counts.rds"
)
vst_counts <- readRDS(
  "dat/counts/vst/WC24_vst_counts.rds"
)

meta$timepoint <- factor(
  meta$timepoint, levels = c(6, 10, 17)
)

cat(
  "Samples:", nrow(meta), "\n",
  "Genes:", nrow(raw_counts), "\n",
  "Genotypes:", paste(levels(factor(meta$genotype)),
                      collapse = ", "), "\n",
  "Timepoints:", paste(levels(meta$timepoint),
                       collapse = ", ")
)
```

### Feature Selection: Likelihood Ratio Test

The LRT is run on D21 control samples only. The full
model includes timepoint; the reduced model is
intercept-only. Genes where timepoint significantly
explains variance are temporally variable.

```{r}
#| label: lrt
#| cache: true

# Subset to D21 controls
ctrl_meta <- meta[meta$genotype == "D21", ]
ctrl_counts <- raw_counts[, ctrl_meta$sample_id]

# DESeq2 LRT
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = ctrl_counts,
  colData   = ctrl_meta,
  design    = ~ timepoint
)
dds <- DESeq(dds, test = "LRT", reduced = ~ 1)
res <- results(dds)
res <- res[order(res$padj), ]

# Significant genes
sig_genes <- rownames(res)[
  !is.na(res$padj) & res$padj < 0.05
]

cat(
  "Significant genes (padj < 0.05):",
  length(sig_genes), "\n",
  "Top 10:", paste(head(sig_genes, 10), collapse = ", ")
)
```

Top 1000 genes (by adjusted p-value) are used for PCA
and trajectory definition; all significant genes are
shown in the heatmap below.

```{r}
#| label: top-genes
n_top <- min(1000, length(sig_genes))
top_genes <- head(rownames(res), n_top)
cat("Genes used for PCA:", n_top)
```

### Temporal Gene Heatmap

Z-scored VST expression of all temporally variable
genes across D21 samples, ordered by timepoint.
Values are winsorized at $\pm 2$.

```{r}
#| label: fig-heatmap
#| fig-cap: >
#|   Heatmap of temporally variable genes in D21
#|   control samples
#| fig-height: 10
#| fig-width: 5

# Z-score and winsorize
hm_mat <- as.matrix(
  vst_counts[sig_genes, ctrl_meta$sample_id]
)
hm_z <- t(scale(t(hm_mat)))
hm_z[hm_z >  2] <-  2
hm_z[hm_z < -2] <- -2

# Column annotation
col_anno <- HeatmapAnnotation(
  Day = factor(
    ctrl_meta[colnames(hm_z), "timepoint"],
    levels = c(6, 10, 17)
  ),
  col = list(Day = c(
    "6"  = "#93C5FD",
    "10" = "#3B82F6",
    "17" = "#1E3A8A"
  )),
  annotation_name_side = "left",
  show_legend = TRUE
)

col_fun <- colorRamp2(
  c(-2, 0, 2), c("#2166AC", "#F7F7F7", "#B2182B")
)

ht <- Heatmap(
  hm_z,
  name              = "Z-score",
  col               = col_fun,
  top_annotation    = col_anno,
  cluster_columns   = FALSE,
  cluster_rows      = TRUE,
  clustering_method_rows = "ward.D2",
  show_row_names    = FALSE,
  show_column_names = TRUE,
  column_split      = factor(
    ctrl_meta[colnames(hm_z), "timepoint"],
    levels = c(6, 10, 17)
  ),
  column_title      = c("Day 6", "Day 10", "Day 17"),
  column_gap        = unit(3, "mm"),
  row_title         = paste0(
    length(sig_genes), " genes"
  ),
  border            = TRUE,
  use_raster        = TRUE
)

draw(ht)
```

### Example Temporal Patterns

Three representative genes illustrating distinct
temporal behaviors identified by the LRT.

```{r}
#| label: fig-lineplot
#| fig-cap: >
#|   Example genes showing three temporal patterns:
#|   declining from Day 6, peaking at Day 10,
#|   and monotonically increasing
#| fig-height: 3.5
#| fig-width: 9

# Mean VST per timepoint for each sig gene
ctrl_vst <- vst_counts[sig_genes, ctrl_meta$sample_id]
tp_num <- as.numeric(
  as.character(ctrl_meta$timepoint)
)

mean_d6  <- rowMeans(ctrl_vst[, tp_num == 6])
mean_d10 <- rowMeans(ctrl_vst[, tp_num == 10])
mean_d17 <- rowMeans(ctrl_vst[, tp_num == 17])

# Classify temporal patterns
peak_early <- names(which(
  mean_d6 > mean_d10 & mean_d10 > mean_d17
))
mono_inc <- names(which(
  mean_d6 < mean_d10 & mean_d10 < mean_d17
))
peak_mid <- names(which(
  mean_d10 > mean_d6 & mean_d10 > mean_d17
))

# Dynamic range for picking representative genes
dyn_range <- apply(
  cbind(mean_d6, mean_d10, mean_d17), 1,
  function(x) max(x) - min(x)
)

gene_early <- peak_early[
  which.max(dyn_range[peak_early])
]
gene_inc <- mono_inc[
  which.max(dyn_range[mono_inc])
]
gene_mid <- peak_mid[
  which.max(dyn_range[peak_mid])
]

example_genes <- c(gene_early, gene_mid, gene_inc)
pattern_names <- c(
  "Declining from Day 6",
  "Peak at Day 10",
  "Monotonic Increase"
)

# Build plot data
plot_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(
  seq_along(example_genes), function(i) {
    g <- example_genes[i]
    data.frame(
      gene      = g,
      pattern   = pattern_names[i],
      timepoint = tp_num,
      expr      = as.numeric(ctrl_vst[g, ]),
      sample    = ctrl_meta$sample_id
    )
  }
))

plot_df$label <- paste0(
  plot_df$pattern, "\n(", plot_df$gene, ")"
)
plot_df$label <- factor(
  plot_df$label,
  levels = unique(plot_df$label)
)

# Compute means
plot_means <- plot_df %>%
  group_by(label, timepoint) %>%
  summarize(
    m  = mean(expr),
    se = sd(expr) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot() +
  geom_point(
    data = plot_df,
    aes(x = timepoint, y = expr),
    color = "#94A3B8", size = 2, alpha = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    data = plot_means,
    aes(x = timepoint, ymin = m - se, ymax = m + se),
    width = 0.8, color = "#1E40AF", linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = plot_means,
    aes(x = timepoint, y = m),
    color = "#1E40AF", linewidth = 1
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = plot_means,
    aes(x = timepoint, y = m),
    color = "#1E40AF", size = 3
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ label, nrow = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(6, 10, 17),
    labels = c("Day 6", "Day 10", "Day 17")
  ) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "VST Expression") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(
      size = 10, face = "bold"
    ),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```

### PCA: Samples in PC Space

PCA is computed on D21 controls using the top LRT
genes. All 18 samples are then projected into this
control-defined space.

```{r}
#| label: fig-pca-3d
#| fig-cap: >
#|   Interactive 3D PCA of all samples projected
#|   into the D21 control-defined PC space

# PCA on D21 controls
pca_input <- t(as.matrix(
  vst_counts[top_genes, ctrl_meta$sample_id]
))
pca_res <- prcomp(pca_input, scale. = TRUE,
                  center = TRUE)

# Variance explained
var_pct <- round(
  summary(pca_res)$importance[2, 1:3] * 100, 1
)

# Project all samples
all_input <- t(as.matrix(
  vst_counts[top_genes, meta$sample_id]
))
all_proj <- predict(pca_res, all_input)

# Build data frame
pca_df <- data.frame(
  PC1       = all_proj[, 1],
  PC2       = all_proj[, 2],
  PC3       = all_proj[, 3],
  sample    = meta$sample_id,
  genotype  = meta$genotype,
  timepoint = paste0("Day ", meta$timepoint)
)

# Color + symbol maps
geno_colors <- c("D21" = "#3B82F6", "T21" = "#EF4444")
tp_symbols  <- c(
  "Day 6" = "circle", "Day 10" = "square",
  "Day 17" = "diamond"
)

plot_ly(
  pca_df,
  x      = ~PC1, y = ~PC2, z = ~PC3,
  color  = ~genotype,
  symbol = ~timepoint,
  colors = geno_colors,
  symbols = tp_symbols,
  type   = "scatter3d",
  mode   = "markers",
  marker = list(size = 6, opacity = 0.85),
  text   = ~paste(
    sample, "<br>",
    genotype, timepoint
  ),
  hoverinfo = "text"
) %>%
  layout(scene = list(
    xaxis = list(title = paste0(
      "PC1 (", var_pct[1], "%)"
    )),
    yaxis = list(title = paste0(
      "PC2 (", var_pct[2], "%)"
    )),
    zaxis = list(title = paste0(
      "PC3 (", var_pct[3], "%)"
    ))
  ))
```

### Reference Vector and Centroid Projection

The reference trajectory is defined by connecting the
D21 Day 6 centroid to the D21 Day 17 centroid in PC
space. Each centroid is the mean of the replicate
coordinates:

$$
\bar{\mathbf{z}}_{\text{Day}\,6}
= \frac{1}{3}\sum_{i=1}^{3}\mathbf{z}_i^{(\text{D21, D6})}
\qquad
\vec{v}
= \bar{\mathbf{z}}_{\text{Day}\,17}
  - \bar{\mathbf{z}}_{\text{Day}\,6}
$$

```{r}
#| label: fig-centroid-3d
#| fig-cap: >
#|   3D PCA with reference vector connecting
#|   D21 Day 6 and Day 17 centroids

# Centroids
tp_num_all <- as.numeric(as.character(meta$timepoint))
d6_idx  <- meta$genotype == "D21" & tp_num_all == 6
d17_idx <- meta$genotype == "D21" & tp_num_all == 17

centroid_d6  <- colMeans(all_proj[d6_idx,  1:3])
centroid_d17 <- colMeans(all_proj[d17_idx, 1:3])
ref_vec <- centroid_d17 - centroid_d6

cat(
  "Day 6 centroid: ",
  paste(round(centroid_d6, 2), collapse = ", "), "\n",
  "Day 17 centroid:",
  paste(round(centroid_d17, 2), collapse = ", "), "\n",
  "Vector magnitude:",
  round(sqrt(sum(ref_vec^2)), 2)
)
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-centroid-3d-plot
#| fig-cap: >
#|   Reference vector (green) from Day 6 to Day 17
#|   centroid. Experimental samples are projected
#|   onto this vector to compute maturation scores.

# Sample points
p <- plot_ly() %>%
  # D21 samples
  add_trace(
    data   = pca_df[pca_df$genotype == "D21", ],
    x = ~PC1, y = ~PC2, z = ~PC3,
    type   = "scatter3d", mode = "markers",
    marker = list(
      size = 5, color = "#3B82F6", opacity = 0.7
    ),
    name = "D21",
    text = ~paste(sample, timepoint),
    hoverinfo = "text"
  ) %>%
  # T21 samples
  add_trace(
    data   = pca_df[pca_df$genotype == "T21", ],
    x = ~PC1, y = ~PC2, z = ~PC3,
    type   = "scatter3d", mode = "markers",
    marker = list(
      size = 5, color = "#EF4444", opacity = 0.7
    ),
    name = "T21",
    text = ~paste(sample, timepoint),
    hoverinfo = "text"
  ) %>%
  # Centroids
  add_trace(
    x = c(centroid_d6[1], centroid_d17[1]),
    y = c(centroid_d6[2], centroid_d17[2]),
    z = c(centroid_d6[3], centroid_d17[3]),
    type   = "scatter3d", mode = "markers",
    marker = list(
      size = 10, color = "#059669",
      symbol = "diamond", opacity = 1
    ),
    name = "Centroids",
    text = c("Day 6 Centroid", "Day 17 Centroid"),
    hoverinfo = "text"
  ) %>%
  # Reference vector line
  add_trace(
    x = c(centroid_d6[1], centroid_d17[1]),
    y = c(centroid_d6[2], centroid_d17[2]),
    z = c(centroid_d6[3], centroid_d17[3]),
    type = "scatter3d", mode = "lines",
    line = list(width = 8, color = "#059669"),
    name = "Reference Vector",
    showlegend = FALSE
  ) %>%
  # Arrow tip (cone)
  add_trace(
    type = "cone",
    x = centroid_d17[1],
    y = centroid_d17[2],
    z = centroid_d17[3],
    u = ref_vec[1] * 0.15,
    v = ref_vec[2] * 0.15,
    w = ref_vec[3] * 0.15,
    sizemode  = "absolute",
    sizeref   = 3,
    anchor    = "tail",
    showscale = FALSE,
    colorscale = list(
      c(0, "#059669"), c(1, "#059669")
    ),
    name = "",
    showlegend = FALSE
  ) %>%
  layout(scene = list(
    xaxis = list(title = paste0(
      "PC1 (", var_pct[1], "%)"
    )),
    yaxis = list(title = paste0(
      "PC2 (", var_pct[2], "%)"
    )),
    zaxis = list(title = paste0(
      "PC3 (", var_pct[3], "%)"
    ))
  ))

p
```

### Maturation Scores

Each sample is projected onto $\vec{v}$ and normalized:

$$
s_i
= \frac{
    \left(\mathbf{z}_i
      - \bar{\mathbf{z}}_{\text{Day}\,6}\right)
    \cdot \vec{v}
  }{
    \lVert\vec{v}\rVert^2
  }
$$

where $s \approx 0$ at Day 6 and $s \approx 1$ at
Day 17 for D21 controls by construction.

```{r}
#| label: scores

# Compute scores
scores <- apply(all_proj[, 1:3], 1, function(z) {
  sum((z - centroid_d6) * ref_vec) / sum(ref_vec^2)
})

score_df <- data.frame(
  sample     = meta$sample_id,
  genotype   = meta$genotype,
  timepoint  = paste0("Day ", meta$timepoint),
  score      = scores
)
score_df$timepoint <- factor(
  score_df$timepoint,
  levels = c("Day 6", "Day 10", "Day 17")
)

# Summary table
score_summary <- score_df %>%
  group_by(genotype, timepoint) %>%
  summarize(
    mean_score = round(mean(score), 3),
    sd         = round(sd(score), 3),
    .groups    = "drop"
  )

knitr::kable(
  score_summary,
  col.names = c(
    "Genotype", "Timepoint", "Mean Score", "SD"
  ),
  caption = "Maturation scores by condition"
)
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-scores
#| fig-cap: >
#|   Maturation scores by genotype and timepoint.
#|   Scores near 0 = Day 6 control level;
#|   scores near 1 = Day 17 control level.
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-width: 6

ggplot(
  score_df,
  aes(x = timepoint, y = score, fill = genotype)
) +
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.5, outlier.shape = NA,
    alpha = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_point(
    position = position_jitterdodge(
      jitter.width = 0.05, dodge.width = 0.5
    ),
    size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8,
    aes(color = genotype)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "D21" = "#3B82F6", "T21" = "#EF4444"
  )) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "D21" = "#2563EB", "T21" = "#DC2626"
  )) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = c(0, 1),
    linetype = "dashed", color = "#94A3B8"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text", x = 0.55, y = 0.03,
    label = "Day 6 ref", color = "#94A3B8",
    size = 3, hjust = 0
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text", x = 0.55, y = 1.03,
    label = "Day 17 ref", color = "#94A3B8",
    size = 3, hjust = 0
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL, y = "Maturation Score",
    fill = "Genotype", color = "Genotype"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "top"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-trajectory
#| fig-cap: >
#|   Differentiation trajectories for D21 and T21
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-width: 5

# Mean trajectory
traj_df <- score_df %>%
  group_by(genotype, timepoint) %>%
  summarize(
    mean_score = mean(score),
    se = sd(score) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot(traj_df, aes(
  x = timepoint, y = mean_score,
  color = genotype, group = genotype
)) +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_score - se,
      ymax = mean_score + se,
      fill = genotype
    ),
    alpha = 0.15, color = NA
  ) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_point(
    data = score_df,
    aes(x = timepoint, y = score),
    alpha = 0.4, size = 1.5
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "D21" = "#3B82F6", "T21" = "#EF4444"
  )) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "D21" = "#3B82F6", "T21" = "#EF4444"
  )) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = c(0, 1),
    linetype = "dashed", color = "#CBD5E1"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL, y = "Maturation Score",
    color = "Genotype", fill = "Genotype"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "top"
  )
```

### Genotype Comparison

```{r}
#| label: comparison

# Compute differences
d21_means <- score_summary %>%
  filter(genotype == "D21") %>%
  select(timepoint, d21 = mean_score)
t21_means <- score_summary %>%
  filter(genotype == "T21") %>%
  select(timepoint, t21 = mean_score)

diff_df <- inner_join(
  d21_means, t21_means, by = "timepoint"
) %>%
  mutate(
    difference = round(t21 - d21, 3),
    pct_diff   = paste0(
      round(difference * 100, 1), "%"
    )
  )

knitr::kable(
  diff_df,
  col.names = c(
    "Timepoint", "D21 Score", "T21 Score",
    "Difference", "% Difference"
  ),
  caption = paste(
    "Maturation score differences",
    "(T21 - D21)"
  )
)
```

Positive differences indicate T21 is further along the
control differentiation trajectory; negative differences
indicate T21 is behind. The results suggest T21 neural
progenitors differentiate faster early but fail to reach
the D21 endpoint by Day 17.

---

*Analysis based on data from Martinez et al. (2024).
Stem Cell Reports 17(6): 1380-1396.*
